title: Avatar: Fire and Ash Third Weekend Box Office Report
text: Avatar: Fire and Ash maintained its dominance at the domestic box office during its third weekend, earning approximately $40 million and reaching a cumulative total of $306 million domestically. The film became just the seventh 2025 movie to pass the $300 million domestic milestone, surpassing Ryan Coogler's horror film Sinners, which grossed $279.6 million in North America. The James Cameron sequel secured the number one position for the third consecutive weekend, despite a 43% drop from its second weekend performance. This follows the pattern established by previous Avatar films, which both topped the charts for seven consecutive weekends. The film is slowing down more rapidly than its predecessors. During their respective third weekends, the original Avatar earned $68.5 million (down 9.4%), while The Way of Water rose 6.5% to $67.4 million. Fire and Ash's steeper decline suggests softer audience retention compared to earlier installments. The overall weekend domestic box office reached $134.7 million, marking the strongest opening weekend of 2026. Upcoming competition from sequels like Greenland 2 and 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple may challenge the film's reign.
